ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Final Inventory & Appraisal
Off Calendar Feb-04-2015 Continued To Apr-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Jew specially appearing for Conchita Lage.

Mr. Jew reported that counsel Michael Walker has past away and that he needs 45 days to assist Ms. Lage in finding new counsel, to go through and assist with filing required documents and stated that he did not know if he would be representing Ms. Lage. The status on filing final inventory and appraisal was continued to April 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the inventory and appraisal would be filed prior to said date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Walker for and with Conchita Lage.

The Court noted that it had the opportunity to review the additional filed documents regarding execution of the will. Mr. Walker stated that there is no opposition from any of the Children, that the Decedent did not own a home, that the home he lived in belongs to Ms. Lage, that about 45-days before his death the Decedent was left with a home and a bank account from the Lottie D. Gates 2001 Living Trust and that the Trust matter has not been filed. The second cause of action/petition to determine internal affairs of a Trust is taken off calendar subject to being re-filed in a Trust action. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary with full authority and no bond (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-01-2014 Continued To Oct-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-03-2014 Continued To Oct-01-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Walker for and with Conchita Lage.

Mr. Walker stated that Ms. Lage resided with the decedent for the last 20 years of his life, that they met in 1981 and resided together since 1994. The Court stated that it is concerned about the will for a variety of reasons. Mr. Walker explained that Mr. Corson took the will into a Realtor's office, that Linda Montalvo is the owner of the Real Estate Company, that Robert Barquero is the Sales Manager and that Rosemarie Montalvo Campos is the Owner's Daughter. Ms. Lage stated that they (Realtors) helped her purchase property when she first came to this Country and that she has a social relationship with them. In addition, Mr. Walker referred to The Estate of Bloch (39 Cal. 2d 570) and The Estate of Archer (193 Cal. App. 3d 238), stated that the will was signed and did not need to be signed on the last page, that they have been in touch with the Children, that he discussed his view with them and that they understand. Furthermore, Mr. Walker argued that the Court has no basis in judging the hand writing, that some of the writing is in cursive and some in handwriting, that there is a difference in the ink, and that the additional writing might be considered a codicil. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to October 1, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to receive something further from the Witnesses explaining what they saw and did, why the different colors of ink and why the Decedent signed under the provision "Turn my body over to U.C.S.F" and "Do not resuscitate".

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Continued To Sep-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
